title: Need for Speed Undercover

gpu: ATI Technologies Inc. AMD Radeon HD 7700
title: Need for Speed Undercover

notes: Runs almost perfectly. Runs out of the box, full graphics, 50-60 FPS and online mode working perfectly (it almost doesn't has players though haha =D). The only problem I have is that everything looks quite darker than it should be, like if it has too much shadows or the bright is too low (when it isn't). In the first cutscenes you almost can't see the man's face. It doesn't makes the game unplayable, just a little bit anoyinng until you get used to it.
title: Need for Speed Undercover

notes:
i've not yet (bothered) tried tweaks, that said, i had to drop the graphics settings quite a lot (except cars i refuse to do that) to bring the fps into a smoothish play.
60fps? don't know. 
vsync had to be turned off as it dropped fps... a lot.
(that said, i've got to google that, many games are having vsync performance issues)
i've witnessed minor but significant texture glitching, change the car tyres to get tyres that try to be white most of the time, or the busted cutscene the people seem to have shaders applied erratically to them, so you end up with half an officer covered in shadow, and what's not, has like paint reflection shading or something.

these are actually VERY minor annoyances, and without any tweaks, if i had more time right now, i'd try the tweaks. got things to do for now, but i'm giving a breakdown now anyway. i'll see what happens with tweaks in a day or so and report back again...
(i thought online was dead... though i've never bothered with online in this sort of game before anyway, so can't and won't test that)

running fedora by the way.
title: Need for Speed Undercover

notes:
so my previous post was actually the "bad" graphics drivers... running the proper amd drivers now, and performance is much better. maxed ALL settings @1080p, EXCEPT world effects (that orange yellow tint is intolerable)
the white tyre glitch is actually a REFLECTION, light hits it and for some reason it lights up the affected area at full tilt... (lighting bug)
the only reason i'm not hitting "platinum" on this game, is that i suspect the lighting thing is actually a proton/wine bug. it'll take a few days of investigation tops to find and fix for full compatibility... probably something that will get fixed by coincidence anyway...
but "platinum" means perfect. and i can't see that being a bug windows had.
besides this minor detail you'll rarely actually have the time to really notice, it seems to run absolutely perfect, no other signs of artifacts, or glitches.
